name: CNH Industrial N.V.

profile.description: CNH Industrial N.V. is a global capital goods company that designs, manufactures, and distributes agricultural and construction equipment under iconic brands including Case IH, New Holland, and Case Construction. Founded in 1842 and headquartered in Basildon, UK. The company is executing a 'Breaking New Ground' strategy combining AI, autonomy, and precision technology with legacy equipment to modernize farming and construction. Q1 2026 revenue was $3.83B (flat YoY) with net income of $10M, sharply down from $132M, as the agricultural equipment downturn continues. CNH expects global ag demand to decline another 5% in 2026.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): CNH has consistently introduced precision agriculture technology — over 15 new tractor launches, 10 harvesting products, and 30+ precision tech releases planned by end of 2027 [1]. The 2022 spin-off of Iveco Group to create a pure-play ag/construction company was a meaningful strategic transformation. AI Deployment (2/2): CNH's 2025 Tech Day showcased an AI Tech Assistant deployed to hundreds of dealer groups for real-time diagnostics, and every member of the global leadership team is running at least one generative or agentic AI pilot [1]. The FieldOps digital platform connects, views, and manages farming operations with AI-enhanced productivity tools [3]. Market Share (1/2): CNH is a meaningful player in global agricultural equipment alongside Deere and AGCO, with solid brand equity in Case IH and New Holland, but is not the category leader [1]. Pipeline (1/2): Specific product roadmap through 2027 with named product lines and the FieldOps platform, though limited revenue visibility on new precision ag products [1]. R&D Intensity (0/1): R&D investment of $1.2B in 2024 is significant in absolute terms but slightly below Deere as a percentage of revenue [3].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): CNH's 'Breaking New Ground' purpose statement connects equipment manufacturing to the existential challenge of feeding a growing world sustainably [3]. CEO Gerrit Marx has explicitly framed this as a generational mission — 'tomorrow's agronomist will be agentic AI helping farmers make bigger decisions better' [1]. This is specific, thoughtful, and clearly shapes R&D investment and product strategy. The 1842 founding provides 180+ years of institutional purpose continuity. Community Impact (1/2): CNH maintains employee foundations and corporate philanthropy programs. The company scored 83/100 on S&P Global's 2025 Corporate Sustainability Assessment and ranks in the top 1% of the 2026 Sustainability Yearbook, with CDP A Climate and A- Water scores [4]. These are substantive programs, though specific dollar amounts for community investment were not documented. Employee Retention (1/2): CNH completed a strategic leadership reorganization in 2025 under CEO Marx (appointed July 2024). The appointment of Carlo Materazzo as Chief Manufacturing Officer overseeing 15 countries and Britton Worthen as Chief Legal Officer suggests a stable, professionalized leadership team [3]. No specific retention or tenure data available. Insider Buying (0/2): No evidence of significant net insider buying in the last 12 months. Dividend yield of 0.93% with a declining dividend from $0.47/share (2024) to $0.25 (2025) to $0.10 (2026) reflects financial constraint rather than insider conviction.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically calculated as Growth sleeve (CNH not in portfolios.json; dividend yield 0.93% < 2% threshold). Liquidity floor check: $10.75 x 15,837,958 = $170.3M — passes easily [5]. D/E of 333.7% is above the 300% floor due to significant financial services debt (score: 0.0, 1x weight). ROE of 4.84% reflects trough earnings — well below the 15% target (score: 0.32, 2x weight). Revenue growth YoY of -0.1% is essentially flat (score: 0.66, 1x weight). 5yr revenue CAGR of -8.4% (2022-2025 3-year proxy) hits the floor (score: 0.0, 2x weight). Forward P/E of 14.74 is well below the 20 target (score: 1.0, 1x weight). Profit margin of 2.13% is thin for any industrial (score: 0.11, 1x weight). Forward PEG of 0.61 is excellent, well below 1.0 target (score: 1.0, 2x weight). Weighted sum: 4.42/10 = 4.4, rounds to 4.

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9 — PROTECTED): CNH manufactures physical heavy equipment — tractors, harvesters, excavators, loaders — that cannot be replaced by AI software [1]. The business involves capital-intensive physical products with significant aftermarket services, 3,600+ dealer networks, and a financial services arm, all requiring physical infrastructure that AI structurally cannot displace. AI is fundamentally an enabler for CNH: precision agriculture tools, AI-powered field analytics, the FieldOps digital platform, and the AI Tech Assistant dealer tool enhance product value and customer retention rather than threatening the business [1][3]. No analysts have flagged AI as a disruption risk for agricultural or construction equipment manufacturers. AI Opportunity (1/1): CNH generates measurable AI-enabled value through its AI Tech Assistant deployed to hundreds of dealer groups, the FieldOps digital platform for farm management, and precision agriculture tools integrated across the product line [1]. Every global leader is running at least one generative or agentic AI pilot program, demonstrating systematic AI revenue contribution.

infinite_game.summary: CNH has a genuine long-term purpose around sustainable agriculture and food security that shapes its R&D investment and product strategy even during a historic equipment downturn. CEO Marx's framing of AI as a 'generational shift' in farming productivity reflects infinite game thinking. The 180+ year company history (Case IH founded 1842) provides institutional evidence of playing to keep playing. However, the severe current downturn tests whether the company can sustain long-term investment when margins compress to 1%.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): CNH's 'Breaking New Ground' purpose connects equipment manufacturing to the existential challenge of feeding 10 billion people sustainably [3]. CEO Marx's vision of agentic AI transforming farming productivity is specific, forward-looking, and clearly shapes R&D allocation even during the worst ag downturn in decades. The 180+ year history provides multi-generational purpose continuity. However, the cause hasn't yet been validated by a defining sacrifice or transformation — CNH continues to make the same fundamental products. Scores 2 points: genuine cause, partially lived.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): CNH completed strategic leadership changes in 2025 under CEO Marx, including appointing Carlo Materazzo (Chief Manufacturing Officer, 15 countries) and Britton Worthen (Chief Legal Officer) [3]. The company maintains 34,197 employees across global operations with institutional stability from 180+ years of operation. However, the recent CEO transition (Marx appointed July 2024) and ongoing margin pressure create organizational uncertainty. Scores 1 point: adequate trust.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): CNH explicitly benchmarks against John Deere, AGCO, and Caterpillar and frames its product roadmap in terms of capability gaps to close rather than competitors to defeat [1]. Management language focuses on delivering better products and improving underlying margins despite the downturn — a self-improvement orientation. The precision ag technology roadmap is clearly inspired by studying competitors' approaches. Scores 1 point.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): CNH executed a major strategic pivot by spinning off Iveco Group in 2022 to create a pure-play agriculture/construction company, simplifying the portfolio and sharpening strategic focus [1]. The ongoing evolution from pure iron manufacturer to tech-integrated 'precision agriculture platform' provider represents meaningful strategic adaptation. The FieldOps digital platform signals intent to evolve beyond hardware. Scores 1 point: some flexibility.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Despite the worst agricultural equipment downturn in decades with Q1 2026 ag EBIT margins compressing to 1%, CNH maintained its R&D investment of $1.2B and continued 15+ new tractor launches and 30+ precision tech releases through 2027 [1][3]. CEO Marx's statement that 'CNH is moving fast to deliver commitments even as markets move slowly' reflects courage to invest through the cycle. The 210-220 bps tariff headwind adds to the difficulty. Scores 1 point: some courageous decisions.

investment_thesis: CNH Industrial is a high-quality franchise navigating a cyclical trough with a forward PEG of 0.61 — one of the most attractive valuations in the industrial sector [5]. Q1 2026 revenue of $3.83B was flat YoY (beating estimates), and the company reaffirmed full-year guidance of flat to -4% industrial net sales with $0.35-$0.45 adjusted EPS [1]. CNH's precision agriculture technology stack — 30+ new product releases planned by 2027, every global leader running AI pilots, and the FieldOps digital platform — positions it to capture greater value per unit as the cycle turns [1][3]. The S&P Global top 1% sustainability ranking and CDP A Climate score add ESG quality [4]. Patient investors are buying a 180-year-old global agricultural equipment leader at cycle-trough multiples with visible recovery expected in 2027.
thesis_continued: The near-term bear case is severe: industry retail demand is forecast down another 5% in 2026, Q1 agriculture adjusted EBIT margin compressed to just 1% from 5.4% YoY, and net income collapsed from $132M to $10M [1]. Tariff costs created 210-220 bps of ag EBIT margin headwind. D/E of 334% is above the infrastructure floor (driven by financial services debt), ROE is just 4.84%, and profit margin is 2.13% — all reflecting a business earning far below its cost of capital at cycle trough [5]. Adjusted EPS guidance of $0.35-$0.45 represents a -94% decline from FY2024. Revenue CAGR of -8.4% over 3 years shows persistent topline contraction. If the agricultural equipment downturn extends beyond 2026, CNH's elevated leverage limits the financial flexibility to sustain R&D investment and technology spending through an extended cycle.
key_catalysts: Agricultural equipment demand recovery beginning in 2027 as dealer inventory destocking completes and farmer income stabilizes, creating significant operating leverage on CNH's fixed-cost base [1]

Precision agriculture technology monetization — FieldOps platform, AI Tech Assistant, and integrated precision tools driving software/service revenue above hardware margins as the installed base grows [3]
Forward PEG of 0.61 represents deep value at cycle trough — any earnings recovery creates rapid multiple expansion as the denominator grows
key_risks: Agricultural equipment demand trough extending beyond 2026: global ag demand forecast down 5% with high-horsepower North America tractors facing 15-20% declines — further deterioration would strain CNH's ability to maintain R&D investment [1]

Tariff escalation on international supply chains: CNH manufactures across the US, UK, Brazil, India, and Europe — the 210-220 bps tariff drag on ag margins is expected to worsen in 2026, compressing already thin profitability [1]

D/E of 334% creates elevated leverage risk: while much of this is financial services debt, the combination of declining earnings and high leverage reduces strategic flexibility if the downturn deepens beyond guidance
